studyName: Genome-Wide Approach to Measure Variant-Based Heritability of Drug Outcome Phenotypes

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, race, age at which tacrolimus, cyclosporine, vancomycin or gentamicin level was measured, serum levels of tacrolimus or cyclosporine, serum measurement of vancomycin or gentamicin trough levels, peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after tacrolimus or cyclosporine serum level measurement, peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after vancomycin or gentamicin trough level measurement, administered dose of tacrolimus, cyclosporine, vancomycin or gentamicin, schedule of tacrolimus, cyclosporine, vancomycin or gentamicin dose every X hour, and case control status of the subject for a drug exposure.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for drug exposure. All subjects are exposed to at least one of the four drugs, and their phenotype data are available.

variables.name: Age_Cyclo
variables.description: Age at which Cyclosporine level was measured

variables.name: Age_Gent
variables.description: Age at which Gentamicin level was measured

variables.name: Age_Tacro
variables.description: Age at which Tacrolimus level was measured

variables.name: Age_Vanco
variables.description: Age at which Vancomycin level was measured

variables.name: Cyclo_Dose
variables.description: Administered dose of cyclosporine

variables.name: Cyclo_PeakCr
variables.description: Peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after cyclosporine serum level measurement

variables.name: Cyclo_level
variables.description: Serum levels of cyclosporine

variables.name: Cyclo_schedule_qhrs
variables.description: Schedule of cyclosporine dose every X hour

variables.name: Gent_Dose
variables.description: Administered dose of gentamicin

variables.name: Gent_PeakCr
variables.description: Peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after gentamicin trough level measurement

variables.name: Gent_level
variables.description: Serum measurement of gentamicin trough levels

variables.name: Gent_schedule_qhrs
variables.description: Schedule of gentamicin dose every X hour

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: Race of participant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

variables.name: Tacro_Dose
variables.description: Administered dose of tacrolimus

variables.name: Tacro_PeakCr
variables.description: Peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after tacrolimus serum level measurement

variables.name: Tacro_level
variables.description: Serum levels of tacrolimus

variables.name: Tacro_schedule_qhrs
variables.description: Schedule of tacrolimus dose every X hour

variables.name: Vanco_Dose
variables.description: Administered dose of vancomycin

variables.name: Vanco_PeakCr
variables.description: Peak serum creatinine 1-14 days after vancomycin trough level measurement

variables.name: Vanco_level
variables.description: Serum measurement of vancomycin trough levels

variables.name: Vanco_schedule_qhrs
variables.description: Schedule of vancomycin dose every X hour

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, analyte type, tumor status, and histological type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, and sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Gender of participant

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
